 {"id":"bd-ff8h","title":"Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery","description":"# Task: Implement GraphQL Batched Repository Discovery

## Purpose
Efficiently discover open issues and PRs across all configured repos using GraphQL alias batching, reducing API calls from O(n) to O(n/25).

## Background: Why GraphQL Batching?
- REST API: 1 call per repo for issues + 1 for PRs = 2n calls
- GraphQL aliases: Query 25 repos in single call = n/25 calls
- For 100 repos: 200 REST calls → 4 GraphQL calls (50× reduction)
- Avoids rate limiting, faster discovery

## Implementation Details

### gh_api_graphql_repo_batch()
```bash
gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
    local chunk="$1"
    local q="query {"
    local i=0

    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
        local name="${repo_id#*/}"

        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
        # Issues with metadata for scoring
        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # PRs with metadata for scoring
        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # Oldest open issue for staleness
        q+=" oldestIssue: issues(states:OPEN, first:1, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:ASC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { createdAt }"
        q+=" }"
        q+=" }"
        ((i++))
    done <<< "$chunk"

    q+=" }"
    
    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### parse_graphql_work_items()
Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:
```bash
parse_graphql_work_items() {
    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
        select(.value.isFork != true) |
        .value as $repo |
        (
            # Issues
            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
        ),
        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

### chunk_repo_ids()
Split repo list into chunks of 25:
```bash
chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift
    local repos=("$@")
    local count=0
    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'
        ((count++))
        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

### discover_work_items()
Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL response with fixture
- Verify chunking works correctly
- Verify archived/forked repos filtered
- Verify TSV parsing handles edge cases
- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single API call for up to 25 repos
- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered
- [ ] Labels extracted for scoring
- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","close_reason":"GraphQL batched discovery implemented: chunk_repo_ids(), gh_api_graphql_repo_batch(), parse_graphql_work_items(), repo_spec_to_github_id(), discover_work_items(). Batches up to 25 repos per API call. Tested: found 2 work items via --dry-run.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ff8h","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.851431049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-fi65","title":"Implement basic mode TUI (gum-based fallback)","description":"Task: Implement Basic Mode TUI (gum-based)

Purpose
-------
Provide a simple but functional TUI when advanced dashboard not available,
using gum for styled output and selection.

When Used
---------
- gum available but not full TUI mode
- User prefers simpler interface
- Debugging/development

Implementation
--------------

show_question_basic_mode()
  Display single question with gum styling:
  
  gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" \
    --border-foreground "#fab387" \
    "Question from: $repo"
  
  echo "$question"
  
  Build options array from JSON
  gum choose "${option_labels[@]}"

show_discovery_summary_basic()
  gum style --border rounded "Discovery Summary"
  echo "Repos with activity: $count"
  echo "Total work items: $items"
  echo "High priority: $high"

show_session_status_basic()
  Simple text status:
  for session in sessions:
    echo "$repo: $state"

basic_mode_loop()
  Sequential question processing:
  while questions pending:
    show_question_basic_mode
    answer = gum choose
    send_answer
    mark_answered

gum_confirm_wrapper()
  with fallback to read prompt:
  
  if GUM_AVAILABLE:
    gum confirm "$prompt"
  else:
    read -rp "$prompt [Y/n] " yn
    case "${yn,,}" in y|yes) return 0 ;; esac

Progress Display
----------------
gum spin --title "Processing..." -- command
gum style --foreground "green" "✓ Done"

Formatting Helpers
------------------
format_priority_badge()
  case priority:
    CRITICAL: gum style --foreground "red" "CRITICAL"
    HIGH: gum style --foreground "orange" "HIGH"
    NORMAL: gum style --foreground "yellow" "NORMAL"
    LOW: gum style --foreground "gray" "LOW"

ANSI Fallback
-------------
If gum not available, use ANSI codes:
- Bold: \033[1m
- Colors: \033[31m (red), etc.
- Box drawing: Unicode ─│┌┐└┘

Testing
-------
- Verify gum styling renders
- Verify choice selection works
- Verify ANSI fallback works
- Test without gum installed

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Questions display with styling
- [ ] Options selectable via gum choose
- [ ] Progress shown with gum spin
- [ ] Works without gum (ANSI fallback)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fi65","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:13.195307516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-gptu","title":"Implement rate-limit governor (adaptive concurrency)","description":"Task: Implement Rate-Limit Governor

Purpose
-------
Dynamically adjust parallelism based on real rate limit data from GitHub
API and model responses, preventing throttling and cascading failures.

Background: Why Adaptive?
-------------------------
Static parallelism is dangerous:
- Too high: hit rate limits, get blocked
- Too low: waste time when limits are fine
- Limits vary by time, account tier, prior usage

Solution: query real limits, adjust dynamically.

Governor State
--------------
GOVERNOR_STATE associative array:
- github_remaining: remaining API calls
- github_reset: when limit resets (epoch)
- model_in_backoff: currently backing off?
- model_backoff_until: when backoff ends
- effective_parallelism: current allowed sessions
- circuit_breaker_open: emergency stop?

Implementation
--------------

start_rate_limit_governor()
  Run in background during review:
  while review lock held:
    update_github_rate_limit
    check_model_rate_limit
    adjust_parallelism
    sleep 30

update_github_rate_limit()
  Query: gh api rate_limit
  Extract: .resources.core.remaining and .reset
  If remaining < 500: log warning, reduce parallelism

check_model_rate_limit()
  Scan session logs for 429/rate limit patterns
  If multiple recent hits:
    Set model_in_backoff = true
    Set backoff_until = now + 60 seconds

adjust_parallelism()
  Start with target = REVIEW_PARALLEL (default 4)
  
  If github_remaining < 1000: target /= 2
  If model_in_backoff: target = 1
  
  Count recent errors in sliding window
  If errors > 5 in 5 minutes:
    Open circuit breaker
    target = 0
    Log emergency pause
  
  Set effective_parallelism = target

can_start_new_session()
  Return false if:
  - circuit_breaker_open
  - model_in_backoff
  - active_sessions >= effective_parallelism
  Otherwise true

Circuit Breaker
---------------
Pattern for handling cascading failures:
- CLOSED: normal operation
- OPEN: too many failures, pause all
- HALF-OPEN: after cooldown, try one (not implemented yet)

Metrics Tracked
---------------
- API calls per minute
- 429 responses per session
- Session error rate
- Effective vs requested parallelism

Integration Points
------------------
- Called before starting new session
- Called in session monitor loop
- Updates TUI status display
- Writes metrics for analytics

Testing
-------
- Mock gh api rate_limit responses
- Simulate 429 responses
- Verify parallelism adjusts
- Verify circuit breaker triggers

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Queries real GitHub rate limits
- [ ] Detects model rate limits from logs
- [ ] Adjusts parallelism dynamically
- [ ] Circuit breaker prevents cascading failures
- [ ] Metrics tracked for analysis","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:47.005795803-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-gptu","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T16:17:00.313011296-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,175 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+#
+# Unit tests: gh_actions execution from review plan (bd-vcr9)
+#
+# shellcheck disable=SC2034  # Variables used by sourced functions
+# shellcheck disable=SC1091  # Sourced files checked separately
+# shellcheck disable=SC2317  # Test functions invoked indirectly via run_test
+
+set -uo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+
+source "$SCRIPT_DIR/test_framework.sh"
+
+source_ru_function "ensure_dir"
+source_ru_function "get_review_state_dir"
+source_ru_function "get_gh_actions_log_file"
+source_ru_function "canonicalize_gh_action"
+source_ru_function "gh_action_already_executed"
+source_ru_function "record_gh_action_log"
+source_ru_function "parse_gh_action_target"
+source_ru_function "execute_gh_action_comment"
+source_ru_function "execute_gh_action_close"
+source_ru_function "execute_gh_action_label"
+source_ru_function "execute_gh_actions"
+
+# Mock logging functions used by execute_gh_actions
+log_step() { :; }
+log_warn() { :; }
+log_error() { :; }
+log_verbose() { :; }
+
+_write_mock_gh() {
+    local bin_dir="$1"
+    local mode="${2:-ok}" # ok|fail_close
+
+    mkdir -p "$bin_dir"
+    cat > "$bin_dir/gh" <<'EOF'
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+set -uo pipefail
+
+if [[ -z "${GH_LOG:-}" ]]; then
+  echo "GH_LOG not set" >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+echo "$*" >> "$GH_LOG"
+
+case "${GH_FAIL_MODE:-}" in
+  fail_close)
+    if [[ "$1 $2" == "issue close" ]]; then
+      echo "simulated failure" >&2
+      exit 1
+    fi
+    ;;
+esac
+
+if [[ "$*" == *"--body-file -"* ]]; then
+  body=$(cat)
+  printf 'BODY:%s\n' "$body" >> "$GH_LOG"
+fi
+exit 0
+EOF
+    chmod +x "$bin_dir/gh"
+
+    case "$mode" in
+        ok) export GH_FAIL_MODE="" ;;
+        fail_close) export GH_FAIL_MODE="fail_close" ;;
+        *) export GH_FAIL_MODE="" ;;
+    esac
+}
+
+test_execute_gh_actions_happy_path_and_idempotent() {
+    local test_name="execute_gh_actions: executes actions and is idempotent"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    # Ensure state is isolated to this test env (avoid inheriting RU_STATE_DIR from outer environment)
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+
+    export GH_LOG="$env_root/gh.log"
+    : > "$GH_LOG"
+
+    local bin_dir="$env_root/bin"
+    _write_mock_gh "$bin_dir" "ok"
+
+    local old_path="$PATH"
+    export PATH="$bin_dir:$PATH"
+
+    local plan_file="$env_root/review-plan.json"
+    cat > "$plan_file" <<'EOF'
+{
+  "schema_version": 1,
+  "repo": "owner/repo",
+  "items": [],
+  "gh_actions": [
+    {"op":"comment","target":"issue#42","body":"Hello\nWorld|X"},
+    {"op":"close","target":"issue#42","reason":"completed"},
+    {"op":"label","target":"issue#42","labels":["fixed-in-main","needs-review"]},
+    {"op":"comment","target":"pr#15","body":"Thanks!"}
+  ]
+}
+EOF
+
+    assert_exit_code 0 execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file" "execute_gh_actions should succeed"
+
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue comment 42 -R owner/repo --body-file -" "issue comment should run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "BODY:Hello" "multiline body should be piped"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "World|X" "body content should be preserved"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue close 42 -R owner/repo --reason completed" "issue close should run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue edit 42 -R owner/repo --add-label fixed-in-main,needs-review" "labels should run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "pr comment 15 -R owner/repo --body-file -" "pr comment should run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "BODY:Thanks!" "pr comment body should be piped"
+
+    local before_lines after_lines
+    before_lines=$(wc -l < "$GH_LOG" | tr -d ' ')
+    assert_exit_code 0 execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file" "second run should still succeed"
+    after_lines=$(wc -l < "$GH_LOG" | tr -d ' ')
+    assert_equals "$before_lines" "$after_lines" "second run should not re-execute gh commands"
+
+    export PATH="$old_path"
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+test_execute_gh_actions_continues_on_failure() {
+    local test_name="execute_gh_actions: continues after a failing action"
+    log_test_start "$test_name"
+
+    local env_root
+    env_root=$(create_test_env)
+
+    export RU_STATE_DIR="$env_root/state/ru"
+
+    export GH_LOG="$env_root/gh.log"
+    : > "$GH_LOG"
+
+    local bin_dir="$env_root/bin"
+    _write_mock_gh "$bin_dir" "fail_close"
+
+    local old_path="$PATH"
+    export PATH="$bin_dir:$PATH"
+
+    local plan_file="$env_root/review-plan.json"
+    cat > "$plan_file" <<'EOF'
+{
+  "schema_version": 1,
+  "repo": "owner/repo",
+  "items": [],
+  "gh_actions": [
+    {"op":"comment","target":"issue#42","body":"First"},
+    {"op":"close","target":"issue#42","reason":"completed"},
+    {"op":"label","target":"issue#42","labels":["fixed-in-main"]},
+    {"op":"comment","target":"pr#15","body":"Last"}
+  ]
+}
+EOF
+
+    assert_exit_code 1 execute_gh_actions "owner/repo" "$plan_file" "should return non-zero when any action fails"
+
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue comment 42 -R owner/repo --body-file -" "comment should run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue close 42 -R owner/repo --reason completed" "close attempted"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "issue edit 42 -R owner/repo --add-label fixed-in-main" "label should still run"
+    assert_file_contains "$GH_LOG" "pr comment 15 -R owner/repo --body-file -" "pr comment should still run"
+
+    export PATH="$old_path"
+    log_test_pass "$test_name"
+}
+
+run_test test_execute_gh_actions_happy_path_and_idempotent
+run_test test_execute_gh_actions_continues_on_failure
+
+print_results
+exit $?
